channel.title: Vulncheck KEV
channel.description: Newly added vulnerabilities to the VulnCheck KEV database

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2024-9537 - ScienceLogic SL1 Unspecified Vulnerability
item.description:
ScienceLogic SL1 (formerly EM7) is affected by an unspecified vulnerability involving an unspecified third-party component.

Date Added to CISA KEV: 2024-10-21T00:00:00Z
Vendor: ScienceLogic
Product: SL1

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2022-4972 - Download Monitor plugin for WordPress REST-API Routes Vulnerability
item.description:
The Download Monitor plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass due to a missing capability check on several REST-API routes related to reporting in versions up to, and including, 4.7.51. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to view user data and other sensitive information intended for administrators.

Vendor: WPChill
Product: Download Monitor plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/download-monitor/download-monitor-4751-missing-authorization-to-unauthenticated-data-export

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2023-7288 - paytium paytium Missing Authorization
item.description:
The Paytium: Mollie payment forms & donations plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized data modification due to a missing capability check on the update_profile_preference function in versions up to, and including, 4.3.7. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access to change plugin settings.

Vendor: paytium
Product: paytium

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/paytium/paytium-mollie-payment-forms-donations-437-missing-authorization-in-update-profile-preference

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2020-36831 - NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress Multiple User privilege/security Functions Vulnerability
item.description:
The NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to authorization bypass due to missing capability checks on multiple user privilege/security functions provided in versions up to, and including 4.3.17. This makes it possible for low-privileged attackers, like subscribers, to perform restricted actions that would be otherwise locked to a administrative-level user.

Vendor: NextScripts
Product: NextScripts: Social Networks Auto-Poster plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/social-networks-auto-poster-facebook-twitter-g/nextscripts-social-networks-auto-poster-4317-missing-authorization

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2016-15042 - Frontend File Manager and N-Media Post Front-end Form plugins for WordPress nm_filemanager_upload_file and nm_postfront_upload_file AJAX Vulnerability
item.description:
The Frontend File Manager (versions < 4.0), N-Media Post Front-end Form (versions < 1.1) plugins for WordPress are vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads due to missing file type validation via the `nm_filemanager_upload_file` and `nm_postfront_upload_file` AJAX actions. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary files on the affected sites server which may make remote code execution possible.

Vendor: N-Media
Product: Frontend File Manager and N-Media Post Front-end Form plugins for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/detail/frontend-file-manager-40-n-media-post-front-end-form-11-arbitrary-file-upload

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2020-36842 - Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress wpvivid_upload_import_files and wpvivid_upload_files Vulnerability
item.description:
The Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file uploads due to a missing capability check on the wpvivid_upload_import_files and wpvivid_upload_files AJAX actions that allows low-level authenticated attackers to upload zip files that can be subsequently extracted. This affects versions up to, and including 0.9.35.

Vendor: WPvivid
Product: Migration, Backup, Staging -WPvivid plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/wpvivid-backuprestore/migration-backup-staging-wpvivid-0935-authenticated-subscriber-arbitrary-file-upload

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2017-20194 - Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress frm_forms_preview AJAX Action Vulnerability
item.description:
The Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Data Exposure in versions up to, and including, 2.05.03 via the frm_forms_preview AJAX action. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to export all of the form entries for a given form.

Vendor: Strategy11 Form Builder
Product: Formidable Form Builder plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/formidable/formidable-form-builder-20503-unauthenticated-information-disclosure

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2019-25216 - Rich Review plugin for WordPress POST Body Update Parameter Vulnerability
item.description:
The Rich Review plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the POST body 'update' parameter in versions up to, and including, 1.7.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

Vendor: Rich Review
Product: Rich Review plugin for WordPress

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/rich-reviews/rich-reviews-174-stored-cross-site-scripting

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2023-7293 - paytium paytium Missing Authorization
item.description:
The Paytium: Mollie payment forms & donations plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized access of data due to a missing capability check on the check_mollie_account_details function in versions up to, and including, 4.3.7. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access to verify the existence of a mollie account.

Vendor: paytium
Product: paytium

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/paytium/paytium-mollie-payment-forms-donations-437-missing-authorization-in-check-mollie-account-details

item.title: [vulncheck] CVE-2023-7291 - paytium paytium Missing Authorization
item.description:
The Paytium: Mollie payment forms & donations plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to a missing capability check on the create_mollie_account function in versions up to, and including, 4.3.7. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers with subscriber-level access to set up a mollie account.

Vendor: paytium
Product: paytium

VulnCheck Reported Exploitation Citations:
  • https://www.wordfence.com/threat-intel/vulnerabilities/wordpress-plugins/paytium/paytium-mollie-payment-forms-donations-437-missing-authorization-in-create-mollie-account
